result.summary: composer.googleapis.com exposes the Cloud Composer HTTP API and the `gcloud composer` CLI; both use Google Cloud credentials, and the REST docs also indicate API key support alongside OAuth 2.0.
result.description: Managed Service for Apache Airflow (formerly Cloud Composer) is Google Cloud’s managed workflow orchestration service based on Apache Airflow. It lets users create and manage Airflow environments for scheduling and monitoring data and infrastructure workflows.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth.setup: For user-based access, sign in with [Google Cloud CLI auth](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth) such as `gcloud auth login` or set up [Application Default Credentials](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/application-default-credentials). For app-to-app access, create a service account in [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), grant it the needed IAM roles for Cloud Composer, and use Google-auth tooling to mint OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens. If you need an OAuth client for your own app, create it in [APIs & Services > Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials).

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: In [Google Cloud Console APIs & Services > Credentials](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials), click **Create credentials** and create an API key. Restrict the key to the Cloud Composer API and the calling app or IPs as needed.

variables.name: project
variables.description: Google Cloud project ID in resource paths like `projects/{project}/locations/{location}`.

variables.name: location
variables.description: Google Cloud region in resource paths like `projects/{project}/locations/{location}`.
